item.title: Dionne Warwick
item.description: Genres: Pop soul, standards Scene: Brill Building Era: 1962–present (primary period: 1962–1971) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Listen to the way Dionne Warwick navigates the melody of “Walk on By” — the intervals are wide, and the phrasing syncopates against Burt Bacharach’s rhythm track in ways that m...

item.title: Laura Nyro
item.description: Genres: Pop soul, singer-songwriter, progressive soul Scene: Brill Building (adjacent), New York City Era: 1966–1997 (primary period: 1967–1971) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The piano on “Timer” (1968) shifts from a ballad’s tenderness to a percussive, almost violent intensity in the space of a breat...

item.title: Smokey Robinson
item.description: Genres: Soul, Pop soul, Doo-wop Scene: Detroit / Motown Records Era: 1955–present (primary period: 1960–1972) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify When Bob Dylan was asked in a 1965 press conference which poets he admired, he listed Smokey Robinson alongside Rimbaud and Allen Ginsberg — a casual remark that ...

item.title: The Beach Boys
item.description: Genres: Surf rock, vocal surf, baroque pop, sunshine pop, psychedelic pop, progressive pop, pop rock Scene: Hawthorne / Hollywood, California Era: 1961–present (primary period: 1963–1970) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The Beach Boys are one instrument made of five throats.

item.title: The Beatles
item.description: Genres: Pop rock, psychedelic pop, art rock Scene: Merseybeat Movement: British beat boom Era: British Invasion (1963–1970) Playlist: FAB RYM · Apple Music · Spotify One chord opens A Hard Day’s Night, and no two musicians have ever agreed how to name it.

item.title: The Supremes
item.description: Genres: Soul, Pop soul, Girl group Scene: Detroit / Motown Records Era: 1959–1977 (classic lineup 1961–1967) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify On April 8, 1964, Diana Ross sang “Where Did Our Love Go” the way she wanted to, high and bright, and Holland-Dozier-Holland made her do it again, lower, in a brea...

item.title: The Temptations
item.description: Genres: Soul, Pop soul, psychedelic soul Scene: Detroit / Motown Records Era: 1960–present (classic era 1964–1971) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The Motown act that best illustrates the label’s capacity for reinvention.

item.title: (1964) "Leader of the Pack"
item.description: The Shangri-LasReleasedSeptember 1964LabelRed Bird RecordsProducerGeorge “Shadow” MortonSongwritersGeorge “Shadow” Morton, Jeff Barry, Ellie GreenwichGenresPrimaryGirl groupBrill BuildingSecondaryTeen popTraditional popListenRYM·Apple Music·Spotify It opens with a single piano chord struck like a to...

item.title: The Ronettes
item.description: Genres: Girl group, Brill Building, Pop soul, Rhythm & blues Era: 1961–1967 RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The Ronettes are the girl group at its most potent, the act that proved what happened when Brill Building craft and Phil Spector’s Wall of Sound met a voice strong enough to ride them both.

item.title: (1963) A Christmas Gift for You from Philles Records
item.description: Various Artists (The Ronettes, The Crystals, Darlene Love, Bob B.
